name: Shared-element navigation via View Transitions (browser-native)
algorithm: If document.startViewTransition is unsupported or reduced motion is set, the injected updateDOM() runs instantly and focus moves to the new view. Otherwise the outgoing element is tagged with a shared view-transition-name, the DOM mutation runs inside startViewTransition so the browser morphs the matching pair, the name is freed once transition.ready settles, and focus moves to the new heading after transition.finished.
reduced_motion: prefers-reduced-motion forces the instant path: DOM updates with no view transition or morph.
responsive_behaviour: fluid; no fixed pixel motion; morph driven by element geometry
browser_support: Same-document view transitions in Chromium 111+ with rollout to Safari/Firefox; everywhere else automatically takes the instant fallback.
accessibility: Morph is pure enhancement; DOM always correct; focus moved to the new heading (tabindex -1 if needed); modifier/middle clicks left to native behaviour.
